title: Link Object Fields to Scorecard via Foreign Key
description:
## Context
The `OBJECT_FIELDS` table was linked to the `SCORECARD` table by object name (e.g., "Account") rather than a proper foreign key. This made it difficult for data architects to trace relationships and degraded query integrity.

## Change
Add a `scorecard_id` foreign key column to the `OBJECT_FIELDS` table, replacing the name-based linkage. The relationship uses CASCADE delete so that removing a scorecard automatically deletes its associated object fields.

The URL routing continues to use the Scorecard API name (not the numeric ID) because external apps will eventually deep-link into DataTools Pro using that name.

## Data Model
- New column: `object_fields.scorecard_id` (FK → `scorecard.id`)
- ON DELETE CASCADE enforced
- Table may be truncated and re-seeded since this is a core schema change.
acceptance_criteria: object_fields table contains a scorecard_id foreign key column referencing scorecard.id
CASCADE delete is configured so removing a scorecard deletes its object_fields rows
URL routing still uses the Scorecard API name rather than numeric ID
Foreign key naming convention is consistent with existing schema conventions
data_model_changes: Added scorecard_id foreign key column to object_fields table with CASCADE delete referencing scorecard.id. Replaced name-based linkage with proper relational key. Table truncated and re-seeded for the schema migration.
implementation_notes: The scorecard_id column was added with CASCADE relationship. Deleting a scorecard now cascades to remove all its object_fields. The object name is kept in URLs for external app deep-linking support. Table truncation was acceptable since this was a fundamental schema change.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the scorecard_id column was added with CASCADE delete and requested review. Discussion covered keeping the object name in URLs for future external-app linking, and agreed on consistent foreign key naming.
